summary: Add the transport-neutral workbench MCP handler for project files, diagnostics, parameters, history, preview, export, and review-gated mutations.

decisions.decision: Keep MCP tool execution transport-neutral and inject the runtime plus engine explicitly; do not claim a desktop process or platform availability from this handler alone.
decisions.rationale: The web and desktop platform seams remain capability-aware, while a shared handler makes Appendix B behavior testable without opening a socket or bypassing platform permissions.

decisions.decision: write_file and set_parameters return pending_review records and do not mutate runtime state until a future in-app approval path consumes them.
decisions.rationale: External agents must not bypass the same diff-review gate required for AI and MCP mutations; this slice establishes the honest pending state before the desktop review UI is wired.

testEvidence.criterion: The MCP handler produces Appendix B-shaped file, parameter, preview, diagnostic, export, history, and pending-review results.
testEvidence.test: Run the focused handler test once after the implementation, lint/provenance checks once, and the production build once after the type correction.

red.outcome: RED: the new fixture asserted an incorrect UTF-8 byte count (test-only expectation defect); the handler and two other cases passed.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/mcp/workbench-mcp-handler.test.ts --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd lint; pnpm.cmd check:provenance; pnpm.cmd build
green.outcome: GREEN: focused handler coverage passed 1 file and 3 tests; lint/source policy and provenance validation passed; TypeScript and Vite production build passed with 235 modules transformed.

questions: Desktop MCP stdio lifecycle, permission UI, screenshot capture, and hosted AC-11.b/c evidence remain pending; export saving remains platform-destination dependent.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
